lastPrompt: # Marketify — Ralph Agent Instructions  You are an autonomous coding agent building **Marketify**, a mobile marketplace where creators claim bounties from listers (Expo + React Native + Supabase). Eac…

input.prompt:
Review the US-045 (submit-video edge function) implementation on the `ralph/marketify-mvp` branch in `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify`.

## Story acceptance criteria (US-045)

From `scripts/ralph/prd.json`:
- POST `{ application_id, video_url, post_condition_affirmations: { [pc_id]: boolean } }`
- Server validates URL shape (TikTok / Instagram regex)
- Calls oEmbed (TikTok public endpoint; Instagram is acknowledged unsupported without Meta app token)
- Inserts a `submissions` row with status='pending', plus the `submission_videos` row
- Returns 422 if any post-condition affirmation is missing / not true
- Returns 404 when the application doesn't exist or is for a different creator
- Returns 403 when the application isn't `approved`
- Returns 409 when a pending/approved submission already exists for that application

## Relevant spec sections

- `docs/tech-architecture.md` §5.7 — submit-video endpoint contract (note: spec names `videos: [...]` array shape; the story AC names a single `video_url` field, and the function follows the story AC per the Ralph "story AC vs spec" convention — see the header comment in `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` for the exact rationale)
- `docs/tech-architecture.md` §3h — oEmbed endpoints (IG needs a Meta app token we don't have; TikTok is unauth)
- `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.7 / §11 — submissions + submission_videos schema
- `docs/tech-architecture.md` §6.3 — eligibility split: PRE = machine-checkable at apply time, POST = attestation at submit time

## Diff scope

Files created / modified in this iteration:

- `supabase/migrations/20260418000000_us_045_submit_video_rpc.sql` — new RPC `public.submit_video_rpc(p_application_id, p_creator_id, p_video_url, p_platform, p_external_id, p_oembed)` returning jsonb. Uses `FOR UPDATE` on applications, returns one of `{submission_id}` or `{error: APPLICATION_NOT_FOUND | APPLICATION_NOT_APPROVED | SUBMISSION_EXISTS}`.
- `src/types/supabase.ts` — regenerated via Supabase MCP `generate_typescript_types` to include the new RPC signature.
- `supabase/functions/_shared/oembed.ts` — new shared helper. `classifyVideoUrl(url)` → `{platform, externalId}` or null; `fetchTikTokOembed(url)` → `{thumbnailUrl, authorHandle, raw}` or null. IG is regex-only.
- `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` — new edge function. Modeled after `supabase/functions/apply-to-listing/index.ts` (which you can read as the canonical template: bearerToken → verifyJwt → role check → req.json → validate → classify → service-role client → application lookup → post-condition affirmation check → oEmbed (TikTok only) → RPC → error envelope mapping).
- `scripts/test/submit-video.ts` — new smoke test with 16 assertions. Assertions 1–3 (auth rejection) run locally; 4–16 gated on `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` which aren't in local `.env` (same limitation as every prior edge-function smoke test in this repo).

## What I want you to verify

1. **Affirmation logic correctness.** The function looks up rows from `listing_conditions` where `listing_version_id = application.listing_version_id AND kind = 'post'`, then requires every condition.id to appear in `post_condition_affirmations` with literal `true`. Is this the right check? Any edge cases (empty conditions, duplicate keys, extra unrelated keys in the payload) that are handled wrong?

2. **Ordering of checks.** The function orders: JWT → role → parse body → classify URL → application lookup → status check → post-condition check → TikTok oEmbed → RPC. Is the oEmbed-after-cheap-checks order correct (doc-comment rationale: don't burn a TikTok network call on requests that will 404/403/422 anyway)?

3. **Error-envelope mapping.** Does each RPC `{error: ...}` code map to the right HTTP status per the story AC? Is there a race where the RPC returns an error the TS-side pre-check should have caught (e.g., APPLICATION_NOT_APPROVED after status was 'approved' on the earlier lookup — benign or bug)?

4. **RPC safety.** The RPC sets `search_path = ''` and schema-qualifies all refs. `FOR UPDATE` is on the applications row; duplicates serialize through it. The partial unique index `submissions_open_uniq` (from earlier migration) is the DB backstop, with `unique_violation` handler mapped to SUBMISSION_EXISTS. Any issue with this pattern?

5. **URL validation.** TikTok canonical + vm/vt/t shortlinks accepted; IG `p/`, `reel/`, `tv/` accepted. External_id extracted for canonical TikTok + all IG URLs. Shortlinks get `external_id = null` (which the `submission_reuse_view` mentioned in the header ignores). Is the regex set complete enough for the MVP? Any obvious obfuscation that bypasses it?

6. **Security surface.** Service-role client is created inside the handler after auth is confirmed. No PII in log lines. The `catch (err)` path in the oEmbed fetch logs `err` — is that a leak risk? (The `_shared/oembed.ts` helper throws fixed-string errors so the object shape is bounded.)

7. **Conventions.** Does the code match the apply-to-listing template (helper placement, CORS, response shape, comment density)?

## Report format

Report ONLY medium and above issues with concrete line refs (file:line). For each: what's wrong, why it matters, and the smallest fix. Skip nits. Under 500 words if possible.

content.text:
I have all the information needed to complete the review. Here is the assessment for each of the seven areas.

---

**Reviewing:** `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts`, `supabase/functions/_shared/oembed.ts`, `supabase/migrations/20260418000000_us_045_submit_video_rpc.sql`, and `scripts/test/submit-video.ts`.

---

## Critical Issues

None found.

---

## Important Issues

**1. Affirmation check passes silently when `listing_version_id` is null — confidence 85**

`supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts:171-173`

The application row is fetched with `.select("id,creator_id,status,listing_version_id")`. If `listing_version_id` is somehow null (e.g. a partially-migrated row), the subsequent `listing_conditions` query at line 188 runs `.eq("listing_version_id", null)`, which PostgREST serialises as `listing_version_id=eq.null`. Supabase/PostgREST treats this as `WHERE listing_version_id IS NULL` only in some driver versions; in others it returns zero rows. Either way the `postConditions` array comes back empty, `missing` stays empty, and the submission proceeds as if there were no post-conditions — effectively bypassing the affirmation gate.

Fix: add an explicit null-guard after the application fetch, mirroring how `apply-to-listing` guards its `current_version_id` (line 162-165 of that file):

```ts
if (!application.listing_version_id) {
  console.error("application missing listing_version_id", applicationId);
  return jsonResponse(500, { error: "DB_ERROR" });
}
```

---

**2. TikTok oEmbed error returns `DB_ERROR` — confidence 82**

`supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts:218-219`

When `fetchTikTokOembed` throws (network failure or non-2xx TikTok response), the handler returns `500 { error: "DB_ERROR" }`. This is the wrong bucket — it has nothing to do with the database. The contract comment at the top of the file (line 17) lists `SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` and `DB_ERROR` as the only 500 codes, neither of which is accurate here. Clients that inspect the error code to decide whether to retry would conflate a TikTok outage with a database failure.

Fix: add a dedicated error code (e.g. `OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE`) to the contract comment and return it here, or at minimum return a generic `SERVER_ERROR`. The contract comment at line 17 should be updated to match.

---

**3. Race window between TS-side status check and RPC status re-check — confidence 80**

`supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts:184-186` and `supabase/migrations/20260418000000_us_045_submit_video_rpc.sql:56-57`

The TS layer reads `application.status` (line 184) and early-returns 403 if not `approved`. The RPC then re-reads and re-checks inside the `FOR UPDATE` lock. If the status was flipped from `approved` to something else (e.g. lister un-approves) between the two reads, the RPC returns `APPLICATION_NOT_APPROVED` and the TS handler correctly maps it to 403 at line 250-252 — so the outcome is correct.

However, the reverse race is a real edge case: status is `pending` at TS-read time, but between lines 185 and the RPC call the lister approves the application. The TS-side check short-circuits at line 185 with 403, even though the application was already approved by the time the RPC would have run. This is an accepted TOCTOU tradeoff in this architecture (the apply-to-listing function has an identical pattern), but it means a creator who was approved mid-submit gets a spurious 403. Worth a code comment so the next reader doesn't "fix" it by removing the RPC re-check.

This is not a correctness bug (no data corruption possible) but it is an observable user-facing glitch in a narrow window. Confidence 80 that it will matter in practice is low for MVP traffic, but the missing comment is a maintainability risk.

Fix: add a comment next to the RPC error mapping block explaining the TOCTOU relationship, matching how `apply-to-listing` documents its LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED race.

---

## Verified Correct

1. **Affirmation logic** — empty conditions array short-circuits the loop cleanly (no false positive). Extra keys in the payload are silently ignored, which is correct per the AC ("every condition must appear with `true`" — it says nothing about disallowing extra keys). `parseAffirmations` correctly rejects non-boolean values before the DB round-trip.

2. **Check ordering** — JWT → role → parse → classify → env check → DB lookup → status → affirmations → oEmbed → RPC is correct. The rationale (avoid burning a TikTok network call on cheap-reject paths) is sound.

3. **Error-envelope mapping** — all three RPC error codes map to the documented HTTP statuses. The `APPLICATION_NOT_APPROVED` race noted above is benign in terms of data integrity.

4. **RPC safety** — `search_path = ''` is set, all identifiers are schema-qualified, `FOR UPDATE` serialises concurrent submits, `unique_violation` is the DB backstop. The partial index `submissions_open_uniq` covers `('pending', 'approved')` which matches the existence check in the RPC (lines 64-65 of the migration). No issue.

5. **URL validation** — the regex set covers the documented formats. For MVP the main missing case is `m.tiktok.com` (mobile web share), but that is not listed in the spec and is not a regression in this iteration.

6. **Security surface** — service-role client created post-auth. The `catch (err)` at line 217 of `index.ts` logs the thrown `Error` object; because `oembed.ts` throws only `new Error("fixed string")` the log payload is bounded to the message string and stack trace, no PII. Acceptable.

7. **Conventions** — matches the `apply-to-listing` template: same `bearerToken` helper, same `jsonResponse` helper, same CORS handling, same service-role client init pattern, same RPC result shape guard.

Fix: add an explicit null-guard after the application fetch, mirroring how `apply-to-listing` guards its `current_version_id` (line 162-165 of that file):

```ts
if (!application.listing_version_id) {
  console.error("application missing listing_version_id", applicationId);
  return jsonResponse(500, { error: "DB_ERROR" });
}
```

---

**2. TikTok oEmbed error returns `DB_ERROR` — confidence 82**

`supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts:218-219`

When `fetchTikTokOembed` throws (network failure or non-2xx TikTok response), the handler returns `500 { error: "DB_ERROR" }`. This is the wrong bucket — it has nothing to do with the database. The contract comment at the top of the file (line 17) lists `SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` and `DB_ERROR` as the only 500 codes, neither of which is accurate here. Clients that inspect the error code to decide whether to retry would conflate a TikTok outage with a database failure.

Fix: add a dedicated error code (e.g. `OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE`) to the contract comment and return it here, or at minimum return a generic `SERVER_ERROR`. The contract comment at line 17 should be updated to match.

---

**3. Race window between TS-side status check and RPC status re-check — confidence 80**

`supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts:184-186` and `supabase/migrations/20260418000000_us_045_submit_video_rpc.sql:56-57`

The TS layer reads `application.status` (line 184) and early-returns 403 if not `approved`. The RPC then re-reads and re-checks inside the `FOR UPDATE` lock. If the status was flipped from `approved` to something else (e.g. lister un-approves) between the two reads, the RPC returns `APPLICATION_NOT_APPROVED` and the TS handler correctly maps it to 403 at line 250-252 — so the outcome is correct.

However, the reverse race is a real edge case: status is `pending` at TS-read time, but between lines 185 and the RPC call the lister approves the application. The TS-side check short-circuits at line 185 with 403, even though the application was already approved by the time the RPC would have run. This is an accepted TOCTOU tradeoff in this architecture (the apply-to-listing function has an identical pattern), but it means a creator who was approved mid-submit gets a spurious 403. Worth a code comment so the next reader doesn't "fix" it by removing the RPC re-check.

This is not a correctness bug (no data corruption possible) but it is an observable user-facing glitch in a narrow window. Confidence 80 that it will matter in practice is low for MVP traffic, but the missing comment is a maintainability risk.

Fix: add a comment next to the RPC error mapping block explaining the TOCTOU relationship, matching how `apply-to-listing` documents its LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED race.

---

## Verified Correct

1. **Affirmation logic** — empty conditions array short-circuits the loop cleanly (no false positive). Extra keys in the payload are silently ignored, which is correct per the AC ("every condition must appear with `true`" — it says nothing about disallowing extra keys). `parseAffirmations` correctly rejects non-boolean values before the DB round-trip.

2. **Check ordering** — JWT → role → parse → classify → env check → DB lookup → status → affirmations → oEmbed → RPC is correct. The rationale (avoid burning a TikTok network call on cheap-reject paths) is sound.

3. **Error-envelope mapping** — all three RPC error codes map to the documented HTTP statuses. The `APPLICATION_NOT_APPROVED` race noted above is benign in terms of data integrity.

4. **RPC safety** — `search_path = ''` is set, all identifiers are schema-qualified, `FOR UPDATE` serialises concurrent submits, `unique_violation` is the DB backstop. The partial index `submissions_open_uniq` covers `('pending', 'approved')` which matches the existence check in the RPC (lines 64-65 of the migration). No issue.

5. **URL validation** — the regex set covers the documented formats. For MVP the main missing case is `m.tiktok.com` (mobile web share), but that is not listed in the spec and is not a regression in this iteration.

6. **Security surface** — service-role client created post-auth. The `catch (err)` at line 217 of `index.ts` logs the thrown `Error` object; because `oembed.ts` throws only `new Error("fixed string")` the log payload is bounded to the message string and stack trace, no PII. Acceptable.

7. **Conventions** — matches the `apply-to-listing` template: same `bearerToken` helper, same `jsonResponse` helper, same CORS handling, same service-role client init pattern, same RPC result shape guard.
Code-reviewer found 3 medium issues. Fixing all three.

740	- Submit hardening: `useRef`-based synchronous lock guards against rapid double-taps (Codebase Pattern #106). `KeyboardAvoidingView` swapped for direct `Keyboard.addListener` driving `paddingBottom` on the inner View — KAV is unreliable inside RN `<Modal>` because the modal opens its own window and KAV's measured frame disagrees with the keyboard's reported frame (new Codebase Pattern). Snap raised from `[0.7]` → `[0.85]` to fit content + ~336px iOS keyboard + CTA.
741	- Files changed:
742	  - `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx` (new, ~340 lines)
743	  - `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` (wired ApplySheet + setTimeout-guarded router.replace; removed US-040 stub toast)
744	  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-042-sheet-empty.png` (new — sheet open, no pitch typed)
745	  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-042-sheet-pitch.png` (new — sheet open with pitch text)
746	  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-042-sheet-with-keyboard.png` (new — sheet at 0.85 with keyboard up, CTA still visible above keyboard)
747	  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-042-submitted.png` (new — Applied tab post-submit)
748	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no schema changes) / mobile-mcp ✓ (4 screenshots on iPhone 17 Pro sim) / DB row 74e23751-1698-4475-aefe-fd09e0cb1fbd verified inserted with `status='pending'`, `cover_note='Quick pitch after refactor.'`
749	- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — (1) hardcoded 336px iOS keyboard fallback removed in favor of trusting the `Keyboard` event (the listener fires reliably post-mount, fallback was a stale workaround from the first verification attempt); (2) stale-closure on `submitting` swapped for `useRef` lock per Pattern #106; (3) `router.replace` wrapped in `setTimeout(350)` so the BottomSheet spring-out plays before the destination mounts; (4) `already_applied` toast variant changed from `'info'` → `'error'` for taxonomy consistency with the other 4 server failure cases; (5) `maxLength={COVER_NOTE_MAX_LEN + 40}` collapsed to exact `COVER_NOTE_MAX_LEN` and the resulting dead `overLimit`/`inputWrapError` style + `remaining` countdown removed.
750	- Spec gap: design.md §4.3 mock shows the counter as `0/160`, but the story AC says 280 chars. Used 280 (story wins for a numeric AC; design mock is illustrative). Resolution: when the design system docs get a real "form copy" inventory pass, reconcile the cap there.
751	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
752	  - **`KeyboardAvoidingView` is the wrong tool inside any `<Modal>`-based component (which includes our `BottomSheet`).** The Modal opens a separate native window; KAV's measured frame and the keyboard's reported frame live in different coordinate spaces, so the sheet content does not get pushed up. Use `Keyboard.addListener('keyboardWillShow'/'keyboardWillHide')` to drive `paddingBottom` on the inner View. Cost is one extra `useEffect` and one piece of state. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
753	  - **Sheet snap-point math must include the keyboard.** A 0.7-snap sheet (596px on iPhone 17 Pro) + 336px iOS keyboard + ~300px form content overflowed by 66px and clipped the CTA. Bumped to 0.85 (724px). Rule: any sheet that contains a focused TextInput needs `snap_point * screen_height >= keyboard_height + content_height + safe_area`. For iPhone 17 Pro that's roughly `0.85` for a one-input form.
754	  - **Reanimated `translateY` does not update the React Native accessibility frame.** During mobile-mcp verification, `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` reported the Send button at its un-translated y-coordinate even though screenshots showed it visibly translated. Workaround: trust the screenshot for visual correctness and use `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` against the on-screen position rather than the a11y-reported position. Don't waste a cycle "debugging" the gap — it is by design (Reanimated runs in the UI thread and does not push frame updates back to a11y).
755	  - **Defer `router.replace` after closing a BottomSheet by ~350ms.** Without the delay, the sheet's spring-out animation plays on top of the destination screen and looks broken. `SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms; 350ms gives a small safety margin. Promoted to Codebase Patterns. Same rule applies for any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path.
756	  - **When the AC and the spec disagree at the wire (field name, error code shape), the spec wins at the API boundary and the UI keeps the user-facing label from the AC.** US-042 AC: `pitch`; spec: `cover_note`. UI label "Pitch (optional)" stays; wire field is `cover_note`. Same precedent as US-041. The "Spec gap" note in prd.json captures the discrepancy so the next person reading the story doesn't think we ignored the AC.
757	---

- Submit hardening: `useRef`-based synchronous lock guards against rapid double-taps (Codebase Pattern #106). `KeyboardAvoidingView` swapped for direct `Keyboard.addListener` driving `paddingBottom` on the inner View — KAV is unreliable inside RN `<Modal>` because the modal opens its own window and KAV's measured frame disagrees with the keyboard's reported frame (new Codebase Pattern). Snap raised from `[0.7]` → `[0.85]` to fit content + ~336px iOS keyboard + CTA.
- Files changed:
  - `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx` (new, ~340 lines)

  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-042-submitted.png` (new — Applied tab post-submit)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no schema changes) / mobile-mcp ✓ (4 screenshots on iPhone 17 Pro sim) / DB row 74e23751-1698-4475-aefe-fd09e0cb1fbd verified inserted with `status='pending'`, `cover_note='Quick pitch after refactor.'`
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — (1) hardcoded 336px iOS keyboard fallback removed in favor of trusting the `Keyboard` event (the listener fires reliably post-mount, fallback was a stale workaround from the first verification attempt); (2) stale-closure on `submitting` swapped for `useRef` lock per Pattern #106; (3) `router.replace` wrapped in `setTimeout(350)` so the BottomSheet spring-out plays before the destination mounts; (4) `already_applied` toast variant changed from `'info'` → `'error'` for taxonomy consistency with the other 4 server failure cases; (5) `maxLength={COVER_NOTE_MAX_LEN + 40}` collapsed to exact `COVER_NOTE_MAX_LEN` and the resulting dead `overLimit`/`inputWrapError` style + `remaining` countdown removed.
- Spec gap: design.md §4.3 mock shows the counter as `0/160`, but the story AC says 280 chars. Used 280 (story wins for a numeric AC; design mock is illustrative). Resolution: when the design system docs get a real "form copy" inventory pass, reconcile the cap there.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **`KeyboardAvoidingView` is the wrong tool inside any `<Modal>`-based component (which includes our `BottomSheet`).** The Modal opens a separate native window; KAV's measured frame and the keyboard's reported frame live in different coordinate spaces, so the sheet content does not get pushed up. Use `Keyboard.addListener('keyboardWillShow'/'keyboardWillHide')` to drive `paddingBottom` on the inner View. Cost is one extra `useEffect` and one piece of state. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - **Sheet snap-point math must include the keyboard.** A 0.7-snap sheet (596px on iPhone 17 Pro) + 336px iOS keyboard + ~300px form content overflowed by 66px and clipped the CTA. Bumped to 0.85 (724px). Rule: any sheet that contains a focused TextInput needs `snap_point * screen_height >= keyboard_height + content_height + safe_area`. For iPhone 17 Pro that's roughly `0.85` for a one-input form.
  - **Reanimated `translateY` does not update the React Native accessibility frame.** During mobile-mcp verification, `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` reported the Send button at its un-translated y-coordinate even though screenshots showed it visibly translated. Workaround: trust the screenshot for visual correctness and use `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` against the on-screen position rather than the a11y-reported position. Don't waste a cycle "debugging" the gap — it is by design (Reanimated runs in the UI thread and does not push frame updates back to a11y).
  - **Defer `router.replace` after closing a BottomSheet by ~350ms.** Without the delay, the sheet's spring-out animation plays on top of the destination screen and looks broken. `SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms; 350ms gives a small safety margin. Promoted to Codebase Patterns. Same rule applies for any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path.
  - **When the AC and the spec disagree at the wire (field name, error code shape), the spec wins at the API boundary and the UI keeps the user-facing label from the AC.** US-042 AC: `pitch`; spec: `cover_note`. UI label "Pitch (optional)" stays; wire field is `cover_note`. Same precedent as US-041. The "Spec gap" note in prd.json captures the discrepancy so the next person reading the story doesn't think we ignored the AC.

## 2026-04-17 21:21 UTC — US-045
- Shipped `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` (POST `{ application_id, video_url, post_condition_affirmations }` → 200 `{ submission_id, platform }`) backed by `public.submit_video_rpc` (migration `20260418000000_us_045_submit_video_rpc.sql`). Contract enforces: Marketify JWT → role=creator → URL classify → application lookup (creator_id + approved gate) → post-condition affirmation check → TikTok oEmbed (cheap checks first) → RPC. Full error envelope documented in the header: 400/401/403/404/409/422/500/502.

Instagram accepted by regex only (IG oEmbed needs a Meta app token we don't have — spec §3h requirement; documented as skipped).
- RPC acquires `FOR UPDATE` lock on the application row; duplicate submits serialize through it and return `SUBMISSION_EXISTS` (409). Partial unique index `submissions_open_uniq` on `(application_id) WHERE status IN ('pending','approved')` is the DB backstop — `unique_violation` handler in the RPC maps to the same code.
- Smoke test `scripts/test/submit-video.ts` covers 16 assertions (auth rejection, role gate, body-shape validation, URL classification, affirmation completeness, approval gate, happy path + duplicate via IG URL to avoid TikTok network). Assertions 1–3 pass locally; 4–16 gated on `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (same local-env limit as every prior edge-function smoke test).
- Files changed:
  - `supabase/migrations/20260418000000_us_045_submit_video_rpc.sql` (new)
  - `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` (new)
  - `supabase/functions/_shared/oembed.ts` (new)
  - `scripts/test/submit-video.ts` (new)
  - `src/types/supabase.ts` (regenerated to include submit_video_rpc)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / smoke test 3/3 always-on ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (no new findings — only pre-existing INFO on `public.events`) / mobile-mcp n/a (edge-function-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — (1) added explicit null-guard on `application.listing_version_id` before the post-condition fetch so a future mis-migrated row can't silently bypass the affirmation gate via an empty `listing_conditions` result; (2) TikTok oEmbed failures now return `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` instead of `500 DB_ERROR` so clients can distinguish upstream outage from a DB bug; (3) documented the TOCTOU race between the TS-side status check and the RPC's `FOR UPDATE` re-check with a comment near the error mapping block.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **Cheap checks before upstream oEmbed network calls.** Don't burn a TikTok rate-limit slot on a request that will 404/403/422 anyway. Order: JWT → role → parse body → shape/classify → DB lookups → status + affirmation gates → oEmbed → RPC. Saves us from having a DDoS-amplifier endpoint if somebody sends unauthed POSTs.
  - **Separate upstream-dependency failures (502) from our own database failures (500) in the error envelope.** Clients that auto-retry benefit from the distinction (upstream is likely retryable, DB_ERROR usually isn't). Worth documenting both codes in the contract comment so a future maintainer doesn't collapse them.
  - **Guard NOT-NULL columns explicitly even when the schema enforces it.** A mis-migrated row shouldn't be able to flip a security-relevant gate (here: the post-condition affirmation check) to a silent pass. One extra null-check is cheaper than a future exploitable bug.
  - **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (below) the oEmbed-upstream 502 pattern; the "classify URL before DB lookup" ordering rule; the listing_version_id defensive null-guard idiom.

content.content: 5:## Codebase Patterns
Apply US-045 migration + regenerate types
Create _shared/oembed.ts helper
Write submit-video edge function
Write scripts/test/submit-video.ts smoke test
Deploy submit-video + run smoke test
Run tsc + expo lint
Spawn code-reviewer subagent
Commit, mark prd.json done, append progress.txt

105	- **Supabase Realtime requires an explicit `supabase.realtime.setAuth(token)` before `channel().subscribe()` under the custom-JWT wrapper pattern.** `src/lib/supabase.ts` overrides `global.fetch` with `marketifyFetch` so HTTP requests carry our HS256 `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` token — but Realtime opens a separate websocket (`/realtime/v1`) that does NOT go through the fetch wrapper. Without `setAuth`, the websocket either auths as anon (no rows visible under RLS) or fails handshake entirely. Pattern: `await supabase.realtime.setAuth(getCachedToken()); channel = supabase.channel(...).on('postgres_changes', ...).subscribe();` then `removeChannel(channel)` in cleanup. Gate the whole block on a `cancelled` flag because setAuth is async and the effect may re-run or unmount before it resolves. Ref: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` US-036 realtime effect.
106	- **Synchronous `useRef` lock for rapid-fire gesture handlers where React state deps would stale-capture.** When a `useCallback` has `[stateFlag, ...]` in its deps and is invoked by a gesture/press that can fire multiple times in the same render tick, the closure captures the PREVIOUS `stateFlag` — two pulls in a row both see `false` and both run. Pattern: pair a `useRef(false)` that the callback reads and writes synchronously (`if (lock.current) return; lock.current = true; ... finally { lock.current = false }`) with the React state that drives UI. Remove the state flag from the callback's deps since the ref is the actual guard. Same stale-capture mechanism as Pattern #82's Reanimated worklets applies to rapid-fire JS-side handlers (pull-to-refresh, double-press submits, scroll-triggered loads). Ref: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` `refreshLock` in US-036.
107	- **Pan gesture coexisting with a parent ScrollView: use `activeOffsetY([15, 9999])` + `failOffsetX([-20, 20])` instead of `activateAfterLongPress`.** Long-press activation is unreliable when the user starts dragging quickly — ScrollView's native gesture wins and the Pan never fires. The threshold-based activation per RNGH canonical docs declares "only downward pulls past 15pt count as pan" without stealing all touches: upward scrolls and horizontal drifts are rejected, so the ScrollView handles normal content scroll, and a deliberate downward pull triggers the Pan. `failOffsetX` prevents a diagonal swipe from being misread as a pull. Inside `.onUpdate`, apply a piecewise-linear damping past threshold (`raw <= THRESHOLD ? raw : THRESHOLD + (raw - THRESHOLD) * 0.35`) for the rubber-band feel. Ref: `RefreshablePlatformCard` in `app/(creator)/profile.tsx`.
108	- **Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function` bundles files exactly as you name them.** The entrypoint MUST be named `source/<entrypoint>.ts` (not `index.ts`) and the `entrypoint_path` field must match (`source/index.ts`). Shared modules live at bundle root as `_shared/<name>.ts` — NOT `source/_shared/<name>.ts`. The entrypoint imports them as `../_shared/<name>.ts` because the bundler resolves relative to `source/`. Mismatched layouts produce "Module not found `_shared/<name>.ts`" at deploy time with no other hint. Canonical reference: any sibling under `supabase/functions/*/index.ts` paired with the deploy `files: [{name: "source/index.ts", ...}, {name: "_shared/jwt.ts", ...}, ...]` — `metrics-refresh` is the earliest example, `get-listing-detail` follows the same layout.
109	- **When a story's AC conflicts with `docs/tech-architecture.md`, the spec wins — but additive fields are defensible when the spec is under-specified.** Ralph rule #1 is "specs win," and that holds for response envelopes, DB column shape, and anything the spec is explicit about. But specs often leave ambiguity at the field level — e.g. §5.4's `failed_conditions: Array<{ metric; required; actual }>` elides `platform` while `min_followers` is ambiguous across TikTok/Instagram. Pattern: align the envelope shape to the spec, but enrich with additive fields when the UI can't disambiguate from spec alone. Document the addition in the edge function's header comment block so future iterations see the rationale. Ref: `get-listing-detail/index.ts` returning `failed_conditions: [{metric, platform, required, actual}]` as a documented extension.
110	- **Concurrency via version-id pin + FOR UPDATE re-read, not engine duplication in PL/pgSQL.** When an edge function needs to "re-evaluate eligibility against the NOW-current listing version" (spec §5.5) without duplicating the TS eligibility engine in SQL, the sanctioned split is: (1) edge fn reads `listings.current_version_id` and runs TS eligibility against it, then (2) calls an RPC that re-reads the same listing under `select ... for update`, compares the locked `current_version_id` to `p_expected_version_id`, and returns `LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED` on mismatch so the client re-opens the detail view. The row lock serializes this flow against `app_private.bump_listing_version` (which runs under its own row lock on the listings UPDATE). Invariant depends on the bump trigger producing a new `current_version_id` for every eligibility-affecting mutation — if a future trigger change lets a condition edit land without bumping, the RPC would silently commit against stale TS eligibility. Document the dependency in the RPC header. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417270000_us_041_apply_to_listing_rpc.sql` + `apply-to-listing/index.ts`.
111	- **Fail-closed applies to malformed data, not just missing data.** Spec §3d's fail-closed rule covers the "null actual metric" case (user hasn't been scraped), but the same principle extends to unreadable THRESHOLD values: a `gte` pre-condition with a NULL or non-numeric `numeric_threshold` must fail the check, not silently pass. Silently returning `null` from the single-condition evaluator for a malformed row is how a future mis-migrated condition flips a listing to "always eligible" without any alarm. Rule: every branch of an eligibility-or-permission evaluator that hits unreadable input returns a fail, never an early "skip." Schema CHECKs should prevent the malformed row from existing, but the evaluator is the last line of defense. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` `evaluatePreCondition`.
112	- **Extract shared modules under `supabase/functions/_shared/` as soon as a second function needs the same logic.** US-039's eligibility engine was inline; US-041 needed the same engine for its server-side re-check. Pattern: extract to `_shared/<name>.ts` with a header comment naming the consumers and the spec sections it implements, have BOTH functions import it via `../_shared/<name>.ts`, and **redeploy every consumer function** in the same iteration — the deployed bundle of a prior function still contains the inlined copy, so a future redeploy of that function without the shared file would fail with `Module not found`. The `deploy_edge_function` `files` array must include every `_shared/*.ts` dependency, not just the new one. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` shared by `get-listing-detail` (v4) + `apply-to-listing` (v2).
113	- **`KeyboardAvoidingView` is unreliable inside an RN `<Modal>` (and therefore inside our `BottomSheet`).** The Modal opens its own native window, so KAV's measured frame and the keyboard's reported frame disagree and the contents do not get pushed up. Replace KAV with a direct `Keyboard.addListener('keyboardWillShow' / 'keyboardWillHide')` (use `keyboardDidShow`/`keyboardDidHide` on Android) that drives `paddingBottom` on the inner `<View>` from `e.endCoordinates.height`. Remember that `BottomSheet` snap point must accommodate `keyboard_height (~336px iOS) + content_height` — for the apply sheet that meant raising `snapPoints` from `[0.7]` to `[0.85]`. Ref: `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx`.
114	- **Defer `router.replace`/`router.push` after dismissing a `BottomSheet` by ~350ms** so the sheet's spring-out animation (`SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms — see `src/design/motion.ts`) plays cleanly before the new screen mounts. Without the delay, the sheet slides down on top of the freshly-rendered destination, which looks broken. The same delay applies to any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path. Ref: `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` `onApplied`.

- **Run cheap checks before upstream oEmbed/scraper calls in edge functions; return `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` (not 500) when the upstream itself fails.** Order inside an endpoint that relies on a third-party URL validator: JWT → role → parse body → regex-classify URL → DB lookups → status/permission gates → affirmation/eligibility check → upstream oEmbed → RPC. - **Defensive null-guards on NOT NULL columns fetched via service-role client, when the row feeds a security-relevant gate.** The database schema can enforce NOT NULL, but a TypeScript read from a future mis-migrated row that returns `null` will silently propagate into downstream logic. When the value gates a security check (e.g. `application.listing_version_id` → the post-condition affirmation check; an empty `.eq('listing_version_id', null)` fetch returns zero rows → no affirmations required → gate bypassed), add an explicit `if (!row.field) { 500 DB_ERROR }`. One extra branch is cheaper than a future exploitable silent-pass. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` listing_version_id guard.
